cards.content: 🪙 Fin: "Meet Rahul. He just bagged a ₹12 LPA tech job! He bought an iPhone on EMI, takes Uber Premium everywhere, and orders Swiggy twice a day."
🪙 Fin: "It's the 28th of the month. Take a guess: What is Rahul's bank balance?"
🪙 Fin: "Spot on. Rahul is what we call 'Premium Broke'. High income, zero wealth."
🪙 Fin: "Here is the brutal truth: Income is just your earning power. It’s what you show off to your relatives. Wealth is the money you secretly *keep*."
🪙 Fin: "If your entire salary goes straight from your boss to your landlord and Swiggy, what does that make you?"
🪙 Fin: "Exactly. Don't be a middleman. Rule #1 of Wealth: Pay Yourself First. The day your salary hits, hide 20% of it in a separate account. Live on the rest."
🪙 Fin: "Final check before I give you your XP. Which habit builds wealth faster?"
🪙 Fin: "Let's test your reflexes! Swipe RIGHT for Needs (Essentials). Swipe LEFT for Wants (Luxuries)."

lessons.title: What is SIP & How It Works

cards.content: 🎉 Scene: You just got placed! The offer letter says "CTC: ₹12 Lakhs". You scream, call your mom, and calculate: 12L / 12 = ₹1 Lakh per month!
🏦 Payday arrives. You check your bank balance. It’s not ₹1 Lakh. It’s ₹78,000. Did the company cheat you?
❌ No. You fell for the "CTC Trap". CTC stands for "Cost to Company". It is NOT your salary.
Your CTC includes things you never see: 1) Employer PF Contribution (Force-saved), 2) Gratuity (Locked for 5 years), 3) Insurance Premiums.
💡 Example: If your CTC is ₹12L, the company might count ₹50,000 for "free office cab" and "free lunch" as part of that ₹12L. Sneaky, right?
⚠️ The "Joining Bonus" Trap: Sometimes they add a ₹1 Lakh joining bonus to inflate the CTC. But if you leave within a year, you must pay it back!
🚨 Reality Check: Always ask HR for the "In-Hand" or "Net Salary" figure before accepting. That is your real reality.
Lesson: CTC is for ego. In-Hand is for paying rent. Know the difference.

lessons.title: The CTC Scam: Decoding Your Offer

cards.content: 💸 So your salary is ₹78k. But wait, why is another ₹3,600 missing? Welcome to "Deductions".
The Big One: EPF (Employee Provident Fund). The government forces you to save 12% of your Basic Salary.
😡 You might feel angry. "Give me my money!" But here is the magic: Your employer MATCHES that amount.
So, that missing money isn't lost. It is growing at ~8% interest, tax-free, for your retirement. It is essentially an automatic wealth machine.
The Annoying One: Professional Tax. This is a state tax (like Maharashtra or Karnataka) just for having a job. Usually ₹200/month.
The Silent One: Group Health Insurance. Your company deducts a premium to cover your hospital bills. This is good, BUT...
⚠️ Trap: If you quit or get fired, your company insurance vanishes instantly. You are left uncovered.
Pro Tip: Buy a small personal health policy (₹5 Lakh cover) immediately. It costs less when you are young.
📅 March Madness: You see seniors running around looking for bills. They are trying to save tax. You don't need to panic yet.
India has two tax systems now: Old Regime (Paperwork heavy) and New Regime (Simpler, lower rates).
🎁 The Gift: Under the New Regime, if you earn up to ₹7 Lakhs/year, your tax is ZERO. Nada. Zilch.
Even if you earn ₹9-10 Lakhs, the New Regime is usually better for freshers because you don't have home loans or kids' fees to claim deductions.
🔍 What is TDS? Tax Deducted at Source. The company cuts tax month-by-month so you don't have to pay a huge lump sum in March.
📄 The Golden Document: Form 16. Your company gives this to you in June. It proves you paid your taxes. NEVER lose it.
Standard Deduction: The government gives a flat ₹75,000 discount on your taxable income. You don't need to do anything to claim it!
⚠️ The Credit Card Trap: Banks will offer you a card with a ₹1 Lakh limit. It feels like "free money".
Scenario: You spend ₹20k on the card. The bill says "Minimum Due: ₹1,000". You pay ₹1,000. The bank charges 40% interest on the rest.
Rule: If you can't afford to buy it twice with cash, you can't afford it on credit.
💰 Your first salary hits: ₹50,000. The temptation? Upgrade your phone, party every weekend, buy new sneakers.
🛑 Stop. If you spend it all, you live paycheck-to-paycheck forever. Use the "50-30-20 Rule".
🏠 50% NEEDS (₹25k): Rent, Electricity, Groceries, Commute. These are non-negotiable. If your rent is ₹20k, you are in trouble.
🎉 30% WANTS (₹15k): Movies, Dining Out, Netflix, Travel. Have fun! You earned it. But when this ₹15k is gone, you stay home.
🚀 20% INVEST (₹10k): This is for "Future You". It goes into Mutual Funds or Gold immediately. Before you spend a rupee.
